name: La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de la Sierra de Montsant – circular desde Cabacés por La Bisbal de Falset
description: Mountain bike the moderate 15.5-mile La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop through Montsant Natural Park's rugged terrain and charming villag
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Cabacés are set within the Serra de Montsant Natural Park, offering diverse landscapes for riders. The region is characterized by spectacular rocky walls, cliffs, and gorges, shaped by centuries of erosion, providing varied terrain. This limestone massif features a steep and rounded relief, with winding paths at the foot of rocky outcrops and through small villages, vineyards, and olive groves. Best mountain bike trails around Cabacés The most popular mountain bike trail is MTB Route 14: Escaladei and La Morera de Montsant Loop, a 17.1 miles (27.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic area around Escaladei and La Morera de Montsant. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tour of Montsant MTB Trail, a difficult 27.5 miles (44.3 km) path. This challenging route offers significant elevation changes and panoramic views across the Montsant massif. Local mountain bikers also love the La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail leading through vineyards and cultural landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Cabacés is defined by rocky walls, cliffs, gorges, and vineyards. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Cabacés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 90 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cabacés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cabacés?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Cabacés, offering a variety of experiences within the stunning Serra de Montsant Natural Park. These routes cater to different skill levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Cabacés?
A: The terrain around Cabacés is characterized by spectacular rocky walls, cliffs, and gorges, shaped by centuries of erosion. You'll find winding paths at the foot of rocky outcrops, through small villages, vineyards, and olive groves. The limestone massif features a steep and rounded relief, providing a diverse and exciting environment for mountain biking.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cabacés?
A: Yes, Cabacés offers several moderate mountain bike trails suitable for families. For instance, the area has 14 moderate routes, which can be a good starting point for family outings. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always clear, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958874637/mtb-route-14-escaladei-and-la-morera-de-montsant-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MTB Route 14: Escaladei and La Morera de Montsant Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer a scenic, moderate ride that could be enjoyable for active families.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Cabacés?
A: Absolutely. Many of the mountain bike trails around Cabacés are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958887780/la-bisbal-de-Falset-and-Cabacés-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 25 km trail that takes you through vineyards and cultural landmarks.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in the Cabacés area?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cabacés offer a range of difficulty levels. You'll find 14 moderate routes, perfect for those looking for a balanced challenge, and 6 difficult routes for experienced riders seeking significant elevation changes and technical sections. There is also 1 easy route available.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking near Cabacés?
A: The Serra de Montsant Natural Park, where Cabacés is located, is rich in natural wonders. You can encounter spectacular rocky walls, cliffs, and gorges. The Montsant River features the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/843527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fraguerau Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l'Ou Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and various caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5086443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in cultural heritage. You might discover ancient hermitages scattered throughout the area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/843525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Bartomeu de Fraguerau&lt;/a&gt;. The famous Cartoixa d'Escaladei, the first Carthusian monastery on the Iberian Peninsula, is also nearby and often features in routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958874637/mtb-route-14-escaladei-and-la-morera-de-montsant-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MTB Route 14: Escaladei and La Morera de Montsant Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cabacés?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cabacés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Serra de Montsant Natural Park, and the well-maintained paths that lead through vineyards and past historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cabacés?
A: Given its location in Catalonia, the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-November) months generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking in Cabacés. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant, making for ideal riding conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Cabacés?
A: While the Serra de Montsant Natural Park is generally welcoming to outdoor enthusiasts, specific regulations regarding dogs on mountain bike trails can vary. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural park areas to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Check local park guidelines for any specific restrictions.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944328028/tour-of-montsant-mtb-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Montsant MTB Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.3 km route with substantial elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40373862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Porrera and vineyards – Porrera loop from el Lloar&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 41 km and features significant climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through vineyards or wine regions?
A: Yes, the mountainous region around Cabacés is renowned as a red wine-producing zone, and many routes wind through picturesque vineyards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958887780/la-bisbal-de-Falset-and-Cabacés-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading directly through these scenic wine-growing areas.
tips: Located in Cabacés, la Covassa, is a cave with two buildings inside, one of which is in ruins.

The hermitage of the Virgen de la Hoya, also known as Ermita de las Nieves, is a building built in two different periods. The first in the 16th century and the second in the 19th century, when the hermitage was enlarged.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking loop is approximately 25 km long with over 800 meters of elevation gain. Komoot estimates it takes around 2 hours and 40 minutes to complete, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this loop as 'moderate' in difficulty. While it's not considered extreme, the route involves significant elevation changes and varied terrain, including unpaved sections. Riders should have good fitness and some mountain biking experience. Beginners might find some segments challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is rugged and varied, characteristic of the Montsant Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, winding paths at the foot of cliffs, and sections through leafy forest areas. Expect some demanding climbs and descents, potentially requiring advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in either La Bisbal de Falset or Cabacés, as both towns serve as excellent starting points for this loop. Look for designated parking areas within the villages. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What natural and historical sights can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers breathtaking scenery through the Montsant Natural Park. You'll pedal between cliffs, through forests, and past vineyards and olive groves. Notable highlights include La Covassa Cave and the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Hoya. The route also provides impressive views of the Sierra de la Figuera mountains.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop utilizes sections of several local paths. You'll ride along parts of the Camí de les Foradades, Camí de Cabacés a la Bisbal, and various segments of the Camí Vell de la Foia. It also briefly touches on the Priorat Extrem route.

faqs.question: Is the La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Parc Natural de la Serra de Montsant, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always carry water for your dog and be prepared to pack out any waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the best times to ride this loop. The weather is milder, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful with spring blooms or autumn colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions, making some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Parc Natural de la Serra de Montsant?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits required or entrance fees to access the trails within the Parc Natural de la Serra de Montsant for activities like mountain biking. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailheads?
faqs.answer: Yes, both La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés are small villages that offer amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and small shops in both towns where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A helmet is mandatory, and protective gear like gloves and knee pads is highly recommended. A GPS device or a downloaded Komoot map is also advisable for navigation.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of the Hoya
